# Big Fluffy Puffy

> Big Fluffy Puffy is a nonprofit web and data project for fireless outdoor culture in the Pacific Northwest.

Big Fluffy Puffy publishes source-linked planning context for campfire and outdoor fire-use restrictions. It is not a government agency. Official land manager sources, posted signs, and current emergency orders control.

The site focuses on Oregon, Washington, and Northern California forests and national parks. Fire restriction data combines monitored official sources, reviewed localized fire-use rules, approximate map geometry, and typical overnight low context.

## Primary Pages

- [Home](https://bigfluffypuffy.org/): Overview of the nonprofit and fireless camp culture mission.
- [Fire Restrictions](https://bigfluffypuffy.org/fire-restrictions): Source-linked current status for monitored forests and parks.
- [Sitemap](https://bigfluffypuffy.org/sitemap.xml): Sitemap index for crawlers.
- [Robots](https://bigfluffypuffy.org/robots.txt): Crawler access policy.

## Useful APIs

- [Monitored Areas](https://bigfluffypuffy.org/api/fire-restrictions/forests): JSON status list for monitored forests and parks.
- [Fire Restriction Map](https://bigfluffypuffy.org/api/fire-restrictions/map): GeoJSON map payload for monitored area status.
- [Place Search](https://bigfluffypuffy.org/api/places/search?q=burnt%20lake): Destination search suggestions.

## Interpretation Notes

- "Unknown" means BFP has not published a confident claim. It does not mean campfires are allowed.
- Approximate BFP map shapes are planning aids, not legal boundaries.
- Source-linked trip checks are best used before a trip, then confirmed against official sources and posted signs.
